name: Display volume with segmentation overlay
description: Tests that the agent uses OverlayData for mask display rather than blending or creating a second volume
prompt: I have a 3D MRI volume stored in variable `mriData` (256x256x128 uint16) and a binary segmentation mask in `tumorMask` (same size, logical). Show the volume with the tumor highlighted in red on top. Make it so I can see through the tumor overlay slightly.

assertions.description: Generated code runs without error in MATLAB
Uses volshow for volume display
Uses OverlayData property for the mask rather than blending
Sets OverlayAlpha to control transparency
Does NOT create two separate volshow calls for volume and mask

name: Display isosurface mesh with vertex coloring
description: Tests that the agent uses images.ui.graphics.Surface with viewer as first positional arg, not isosurface+patch
prompt: I have a triangulation object `tri` from a CT scan isosurface extraction and want to display it in 3D with colors mapped to the z-coordinate of each vertex. Use a perceptually uniform colormap.

assertions.description: Generated code runs without error in MATLAB (with setup: tri = triangulation(delaunay(meshgrid(1:10,1:10)), rand(100,3))
Uses images.ui.graphics.Surface for mesh display
Passes viewer as the first positional argument to Surface constructor (not Parent=viewer)
Creates a viewer3d for the scene
Does NOT use legacy isosurface+patch approach

name: Build app that streams volume data
description: Tests that the agent reuses volume objects and uses waitfor for synchronization
prompt: Create a simple MATLAB app that displays a 3D volume and has a 'Load Next' button. Each click should load the next volume from a cell array of volumes without recreating the display. The app should wait for rendering to complete before re-enabling the button.

assertions.description: Generated code passes static analysis (checkcode)
Creates viewer3d with a UI parent (uigridlayout or uifigure)
Reuses volume object by updating Data property rather than recreating volshow
Uses waitfor on viewer Busy property for synchronization
Does NOT call clear on the viewer or recreate volshow each time

name: Build UI for volume animation generation
description: Tests that the agent builds an app with animation controls and uses getframe(viewer) for frame capture
prompt: Build a MATLAB app that lets the user rotate a volume and record an animation. It should have a Start/Stop Recording button and an Export button that saves the captured frames as an AVI video. The rotation should animate the camera around the volume.

assertions.description: Generated code passes static analysis (checkcode)
Uses viewer3d for 3D display
Uses getframe(viewer) to capture frames, not getframe(gcf) or saveas
Does NOT use getframe(gcf) which bypasses viewer async handling
Uses VideoWriter or movie2avi for AVI export
